mission.title: cloud-native-network: Implemented seccomp and pass the integration test
mission.description:
Fix #25 

So this turns out to be simpler than I thought, thanks to the existance of `libseccomp`. `runc` also uses `libseccomp` which does all the heavy lifting.

One concern I have is the `seccomp-sys` repo. We don't have a lot of choices for a rust binding for libseccomp and this one looks reasonable. However, it is LGPL, so we may want to roll our own bindings. I used this repo just for prototype. If we are NOT OK with LGPL code into our repo, then we should discuss on what is the right thing to do here.

Regardless, this is a functional prototypes now, so I would like you guys to take a look. The PR can be merged as is, given the issue mentioned above.

TODO:
- [ ] `oci-spec-rs` is missing a few fields for seccomp. For example, the default error code is hardcoded to EPERM at the moment.
- [ ] Our own libssecomp bindings??

resolution.summary:
@utam0k Hi.
A couple of things are still missing:
1. The definition of the system calls allowed for each architecture in the form of a collection of strings
2. Integrate the filter definition when starting the container process.
Something like [this](https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/blob/654f3319762c896e1f0b23eef0d2cd70fdd6b9b1/libcontainer/standard_init_linux.go#L161)
3. Add tests
4. Try to better define the constants, files and seccomp folder if possible

I would love to receive a hand from you, the topic is not too simple. Feel free to look on the points I have marked and others as well if you feel appropriate and / or necessary.
steps: The definition of the system calls allowed for each architecture in the form of a collection of strings
Integrate the filter definition when starting the container process.
Try to better define the constants, files and seccomp folder if possible
